paper_title: Evaluation of DFT Method with PBE and optB88-vdW Functionals

methods.description: This method assesses the reliability of DFT methods by evaluating the quality or accuracy of the obtained DFT values through various error metrics compared to experimental data.

steps.description: Select appropriate experimental data as reference values for error analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select experimental reference data for error analysis

steps.description: Calculate absolute error (AE) for each DFT value using the selected reference values.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate absolute error for DFT values

steps.description: Compute mean absolute error (MAE) to describe the mean deviation in the magnitudes of DFT values.
steps.searchable_summary: compute mean absolute error for DFT values

steps.description: Calculate relative error (RE) or percentage error (PE) for each DFT value.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative error for DFT values

steps.description: Compute mean absolute relative error (MARE) or mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) for the dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: compute mean absolute relative error for DFT values

steps.description: Calculate standard deviation to reflect the discreteness of the dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate standard deviation of error values

steps.description: Determine 95% confidence limit for the dataset to estimate expected accuracy for additional calculations.
steps.searchable_summary: determine 95% confidence limit for error analysis
